note_1.title: Nature of Operations and Summary of Significant Accounting Policies

nature_of_operations.description: EOS Worldwide Franchising, LLC was organized on January 11, 2021 under the laws of Delaware as a limited liability company. It is a wholly owned subsidiary of EOS HoldCo, LLC (the Parent), disregarded for federal income tax purposes.
nature_of_operations.affiliate_description: EOS Worldwide, LLC (the Affiliate) combines timeless business principles with practical tools to help entrepreneurs. The Affiliate licenses to the Company the right to use EOS trademarks and intellectual property under a ninety-nine year license agreement and to further sublicense them.
nature_of_operations.duration: Perpetuity, unless sooner dissolved in accordance with the operating agreement and applicable law.

policies.use_of_estimates: Management makes estimates and assumptions that affect reported amounts of assets, liabilities, and revenues/expenses. Actual results could differ.
policies.cash: Bank deposit accounts may exceed federally insured limits at times. Company has not experienced any losses. Not exposed to significant credit risk on cash.

revenue_recognition.standard: ASC Topic 606, Revenue from Contracts with Customers
revenue_recognition.method: Five-step model: (1) identify contract, (2) identify performance obligations, (3) determine transaction price, (4) allocate price, (5) recognize when/as obligations satisfied
revenue_recognition.transfer_point: When control of promised goods or service is transferred to customer

categories.memberships_subscriptions: Billed monthly upfront at subscription renewal date. Revenue recognized at renewal date. Membership periods overlapping year-end are reclassified to deferred revenue. Typically recognized within one year.
categories.boot_camp: Billed and received as part of initial franchise agreement with new franchisees. Carried as deferred revenue until franchisee attends Boot Camp training.
categories.store_income: Revenue from selling training materials, educational literature and other products through website. Recognized upon shipment (when customer obtains control). Company has elected to treat shipping/handling as costs to fulfill the associated product, not as a separate performance obligation.
categories.warm_lead_referral: Revenue from referring general inquiries for coaching services to franchisees. Once member holds initial meeting and relationship is deemed to have substance, Company records revenue for the referral fee. Revenue is deferred and recognized monthly pro rata over the life of the agreement, typically 45 months.
categories.franchise_fee: Company bills and receives revenue associated with new franchisee agreements. Revenue is deferred and recognized monthly pro rata over the life of the agreement, typically 45 months.
categories.practice_management: Revenue from franchisee network offering monthly subscription-based learning management program. Recognized upon receipt with no deferred revenue exists for this stream due to recurring monthly membership periods.

contract_assets_and_liabilities.description: Timing of revenue recognized may not align with right to invoice. Accounts receivable recorded when Company has unconditional right to issue invoice and receive payment. If revenue not yet been recognized, a contract liability (deferred revenue) is recorded. If revenue recognized in advance of right to invoice, a contract asset is recorded.
contract_assets_and_liabilities.transition_note: As of January 1, 2023, Company recognized approximately $1,420,000 of accounts receivable and $1,351,000 of deferred revenue.

policies.advertising: Charged to expense as incurred. Approximately $1,051,000 (2024) and $873,000 (2023).
policies.variable_interest_entity: Company has elected Accounting Standards Update (ASU) 2018-17 which allows all legal entities under common control to elect not to apply the variable interest entity (VIE) consolidation guidance.

income_taxes.treatment: Allocated share of income or loss includable in income tax returns of members. Income taxes are not reflected in Company's financial statements.
income_taxes.uncertain_tax_positions: ASC 740-10-25. Company follows provisions requiring that realization of uncertain income tax position must be more likely than not (>50% likelihood). Company does not believe there are any uncertain tax positions. No interest or penalties were included in operations for 2024 and 2023.
income_taxes.tax_examination_status: Company is no longer subject to tax examinations by US federal, state or local tax authorities for the years before 2019. With few exceptions.

policies.subsequent_events: Company has performed evaluation of subsequent events through March 24, 2025 (2024 report) and March 20, 2024 (2023 report), the date financial statements were available to be issued.

expense_sharing_agreement.description: Company has entered into an expense sharing agreement with the Affiliate. The Affiliate pays all expenses on behalf of the Company and allocates expenses, including payroll, advertising, information technology, interest and other operating expenses, to the Company based on the terms stipulated in the expense sharing agreement.
expense_sharing_agreement.netting: The Affiliate may offset those expenses against amounts it may collect on behalf of the Company and the balance is transferred in cash between the Company and the Affiliate periodically for cash flow purposes.
expense_sharing_agreement.outstanding_due_from_affiliate: Outstanding that is due from the Affiliate under this arrangement

note_purchase_agreement.description: Company has granted a security interest in all assets of the Company per the terms of a note purchase agreement among the Company, Parent, Affiliate, and other affiliates of the Company, collectively as the borrowers, and other third parties.
note_purchase_agreement.balance_sheet_treatment: No amount due is reflected on the accompanying balance sheets.
note_purchase_agreement.notes: The note secures all assets of the Company. Balance has increased significantly ($2.4M to $17.9M) over two years.

note_3.title: Member's Equity
note_3.description: The Parent owns 100% of the membership interests of the Company.

note_4.title: Concentration of Credit Risk
note_4.description: Company's financial instruments exposed to concentrations of credit risk consist primarily of cash and cash equivalents. Company places its cash and cash equivalents with one financial institution. At times, such amounts may be in excess of the FDIC insured limit. The Company has never experienced any losses related to these balances.

key_findings: Revenue recognition follows ASC 606 with five distinct revenue streams, each with different recognition timing
Warm lead referral and franchise fee revenue are deferred and recognized over ~45 months — long tail
Boot Camp revenue is deferred until training is attended — significant deferred revenue driver
All expenses are allocated from the Affiliate via expense sharing agreement — not directly incurred
Note purchase agreement balance has grown from ~$2.4M (2022) to ~$17.9M (2024) — 7.4x increase in 2 years
Security interest on ALL company assets for the note purchase agreement
Entity is disregarded for federal income tax — no tax provision in financial statements
No depreciation, amortization, or impairment — no depreciable assets exist
No lease accounting — no leases disclosed
